component.name: GP Client
component.summary: Connect to GlobalProtect VPN with smart cards

description.p: A graphical client for GlobalProtect VPN. Sign in with a smart card / PKCS#11 certificate (such as a YubiKey PIV), SAML single sign-on, or a username and password.
Save multiple connections in an encrypted vault, unlock it from your keyring, and manage the VPN from a system-tray icon with desktop notifications.
A community fork of yuezk/GlobalProtect-openconnect, under the GPL-3.0.

screenshot.caption: Connect to a GlobalProtect gateway from the main window
Protect your connections with an encrypted vault
Configure the connection and smart-card options
General settings, autostart and tray behaviour
Check for updates from the About page

description.p: The client now reaches the privileged backend exclusively over the D-Bus system service; the loopback WebSocket transport has been removed. Reconnecting after resume from sleep is fast and leak-proof: the gateway's host route is re-pinned to the physical interface before reconnecting (the NIC flap drops it), so the tunnel comes back in seconds instead of stalling on a dead route for minutes, and nothing leaks while it is down since the tunnel is never torn down. Continues the sunset toward the standalone GP Client. Note: requires the 1.4.0 backend.

Backend groundwork for the upcoming independent GP Client GUI: gpservice can now run authentication (prelogin, smart-card mTLS, SAML, and gateway login) itself, driven over the wire protocol. No change to how the current app connects.

The client now reconnects immediately on resume from sleep (reusing the existing session) and shows an honest "Reconnecting…" state, instead of claiming Connected while the tunnel is dead. Fixed the remaining daily SSO crash (the sign-in window was created and revealed off the GTK main thread). Ported upstream 2.6.x improvements: host-id is sent so gateway SAML gets its authentication-override cookie, a portal-cookie cache lets CLI reconnects skip SAML, more robust external-browser sign-in, and Microsoft Defender detection in the HIP report. Note: GUI and backend must both be 1.3.0 or newer.
Fixed an intermittent crash when the window was revealed from the tray or by a second launch — the window was shown from a background thread, which GTK does not allow.

Fixed the window not coming to the front when opened from the tray icon or the tray menu — it now reliably raises and takes focus, including on Wayland compositors like COSMIC where a plain focus request was being ignored.

Fixed smart-card reconnects failing with "data not available" on Flatpak (the backend now restarts cleanly between sessions for a fresh card read), and disabled accidental zoom (Ctrl+scroll / pinch) in the fixed-size windows.
Fixed "Start minimized" being ignored at login — with the option off, the app now shows its window when it autostarts instead of always starting hidden in the tray.
Fixed "Update all" so it moves the backend to the latest version too — previously it re-installed the app's current version, leaving the backend behind on atomic systems until a second run.

Fixed the relaunch-from-tray crash on Flatpak (the 1.2.4 fix didn't work inside the sandbox), and the VPN tunnel is now torn down if the app quits or crashes while connected so tun0 is never left dangling.

Fixed smart-card (PKCS#11 / YubiKey) connections failing with "data not available" after a card re-seat, pcscd restart, or suspend/resume — the backend now recovers automatically without a service restart.
Fixed a crash when relaunching the app from the launcher while it was still running in the tray — it now reveals the existing window instead.
One "Update all" button (replacing the separate app/backend buttons) that narrates each step and offers a Restart/Reboot button to apply the update.
Under Flatpak, the About screen now reads the backend version and install type from the host (they showed "?" / "Flatpak" before).
Fixed the main-screen connection timer (was stuck at 00:00:00) and stopped the tray menu from showing long error text.
Webkit-free backend: the SAML SSO webview now runs in-process in the GUI, so the backend package no longer needs libwebkit2gtk. SSO is remembered across reconnects within a session.
Fixed the main-screen Update banner button (it did nothing); main window is slightly taller so the banner no longer makes the content scroll.
Update-available badge (app and backend) with a startup check; redesigned About showing app + backend side by side; fixed "Update backend" on atomic/rpm-ostree systems.
Shared gp-protocol wire crate with negotiated version ranges so the GUI and backend can't drift; clearer update/downgrade guidance on mismatch; quieter gpservice journal.
Ubuntu 26.04 apt repo (openSUSE Build Service); deb install smoketest gating releases; per-distro Install docs.
Packaging: Fedora COPR now also builds for Enterprise Linux 10 (RHEL/AlmaLinux/Rocky 10); COPR publish gated on the RPM install test.
Patch-tolerant backend compatibility warning; store screenshots; Fedora COPR packaging; Nix flake builds from source.
In-app update downloads and reinstalls the new Flatpak; tidier update UI.
Flatpak: run the bundled gpauth for SSO; clearer backend-install progress.
Initial fork release: smart-card prelogin, Tauri GUI, tray client.
